content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: UnitedHealthcare member service\
  \ request form
- url: https://memberforms.uhc.com/Memberappealsandgrievances.html
\
  - source: UnitedHealthcare
- domain: memberforms.uhc.com

## Content

### Examples\
  \ of requests this form can be used for

This UnitedHealthcare member service\
  \ request form may be used, if available within your plan, to file an appeal or\
  \ grievance for a pre-service denied medical or pharmacy authorization, or to file\
  \ an appeal or grievance for a post-service denied medical or pharmacy claim. It\
  \ may also be used to request Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) documents\
  \ regarding the handling of a claim denial, a copy of the summary plan description,\
  \ or a reference to specific plan provisions.

This form is not for Behavioral\
  \ Health, Dental or Vision services, except for Individual Family Plans.

###\
  \ Legal representative

If you are submitting as an authorized representative,\
  \ a Designation of Authorized Representative Form is required and will need to be\
  \ uploaded. Legal representatives must also provide a copy of a health care power\
  \ of attorney or other relevant document that grants the applicable legal authority.\
  \ If you are a parent or guardian signing and submitting on behalf of a child, an\
  \ Authorized Representative Form may not be required.

### Appeal definition
\
  
An appeal is a timely request by a member, an authorized representative or a contracted\
  \ provider to change a plan denial decision made by UnitedHealthcare.

For there\
  \ to be an appeal, UnitedHealthcare must have communicated a denial or adverse decision\
  \ to the member or provider. If you aren’t sure if your request meets the definition\
  \ of an appeal, please sign in to member.uhc.com to message UnitedHealthcare.

\
  ### Pre-service appeal information needed

For a pre-service appeal, the information\
  \ needed includes:

- Provider name and Tax Identification Number (TIN)
- Authorization\
  \ number
- Date the authorization was requested

This information can be found\
  \ on the pre-service denial letter for medical coverage or prescription drug coverage.
\
  
### Processed claim appeal information needed

For a processed claim appeal,\
  \ the information needed includes:

- Provider name and Tax Identification Number\
  \ (TIN)
- Claim ID number
- Date of service
- Charge amount

This information\
  \ can be found on your claim, bill, Explanation of Benefits, or on member.uhc.com\
  \ in the Claims & Accounts section.

### Special grievance and review information\
  \ shown on the form

The form notes that if a grievance involves an imminent and\
  \ serious threat to the health of the patient, state law requires immediate notification\
  \ of the right to contact the Department of Managed Health Care. The toll-free number\
  \ listed is 1-888-466-2219, with a TDD line at 1-877-688-9891 for the hearing and\
  \ speech impaired.

For Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) Program members,\
  \ the form states that grievance rights are through the Office of Personnel Management\
  \ instead of the Department of Managed Health Care. The FEHB Program brochure states\
  \ that a member may ask OPM to review the denial after asking UnitedHealthcare to\
  \ reconsider the initial denial or refusal. OPM will determine whether UnitedHealthcare\
  \ correctly applied the terms of the contract when denying the claim or service\
  \ request.

### Form flow

The form flow includes email verification, subscriber\
  \ information, patient information, request type, provider information, submission\
  \ details, supporting documentation, and review and submit.
"
metadata: {}
title: UnitedHealthcare member service request form
url: https://memberforms.uhc.com/Memberappealsandgrievances.html

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: External Review | HealthCare.gov
\
  - url: https://www.healthcare.gov/appeal-insurance-company-decision/external-review/
\
  - source: HealthCare.gov
- domain: healthcare.gov

## Content

### Appealing\
  \ a health plan decision

HealthCare.gov states that it is an official website\
  \ of the United States government. Official websites use .gov, and a .gov website\
  \ belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov\
  \ websites use HTTPS.

### External Review

There are 2 steps in the external\
  \ review process:

1. You file an external review. You must file a written request\
  \ for an external review within four months after the date you receive a notice\
  \ or final determination from your insurer that your claim has been denied.
2.\
  \ An external reviewer issues a final decision. An external review either upholds\
  \ your insurer’s decision or decides in your favor. Your insurer is required\
  \ by law to accept the external reviewer’s decision.

### Types of denials\
  \ that can go to external review

The following types of denials can go to external\
  \ review:

- Any denial that involves medical judgment where you or your provider\
  \ may disagree with the health insurance plan.
- Any denial that involves a determination\
  \ that a treatment is experimental or investigational.
- Cancellation of coverage\
  \ based on your insurer’s claim that you gave false or incomplete information\
  \ when you applied for coverage.

### Rights in an external review

Insurance\
  \ companies in all states must offer an external review process that meets federal\
  \ consumer protection standards.

State process: Your state may have an external\
  \ review process that meets or goes beyond these standards. If so, insurance companies\
  \ in your state will follow your state’s external review processes. You’\
  ll get all the protections outlined in that process.

Federal process: If your\
  \ state doesn’t have an external review process that meets the minimum consumer\
  \ protection standards, the federal government’s Department of Health and Human\
  \ Services (HHS) will oversee an external review process for health insurance companies\
  \ in your state.

Depending on your plan and where you live, insurance companies\
  \ may choose to participate in an HHS-administered process or contract with independent\
  \ review organizations. If your plan doesn’t participate in a state or HHS-Administered\
  \ Federal External Review Process, your health plan must contract with an independent\
  \ review organization.

### How to learn more about your state’s external\
  \ review

Look at the information on your Explanation of Benefits (EOB) or on\
  \ the final denial of the internal appeal by your health plan. It will give you\
  \ the contact information for the organization that will handle your external review.\
  \ You can also see the state list maintained by the HHS Center for Consumer Information\
  \ & Insurance Oversight.

### Review timing

Standard external reviews are decided\
  \ as soon as possible, no later than 45 days after the request was received. Expedited\
  \ external reviews are decided as soon as possible, no later than 72 hours, or less,\
  \ depending on the medical urgency of the case, after the request was received.
\
  
### HHS-Administered Federal External Review Process

If your health insurance\
  \ company participates in the HHS-Administered Federal External Review Process,\
  \ HealthCare.gov lists these request options:

- Visit externalappeal.cms.gov.\
  \ The secure website is the preferred method of submission for claimants who are\
  \ able to use it.
- Call toll free 1-888-866-6205 to request an external review\
  \ request form, then fax the request to 1-888-866-6190.
- Mail an external review\
  \ request form to MAXIMUS Federal Services, 3750 Monroe Avenue, Suite 705, Pittsford,\
  \ NY 14534.
"
metadata: {}
title: External Review | HealthCare.gov
url: https://www.healthcare.gov/appeal-insurance-company-decision/external-review/

Yes. Based on your UnitedHealthcare status, **your internal plan-level appeal is already complete and the denial was upheld**. Because the denial is for **medical necessity** and the final decision includes external-review rights, your next step is generally to request an **external review**, not file another standard internal appeal.

### What to do now

1. **Check the final denial letter/EOB**
   - Use the external-review instructions and organization listed there.
   - Confirm the filing deadline. Your account shows **October 28, 2026**, but follow the date and instructions in the notice.

2. **Submit the external-review request**
   - Include the claim number: **UHC-CLM-842917**
   - Attach the final denial decision, EOB, medical records, clinical notes, relevant test results, treatment history, and a letter from your provider explaining why the service was medically necessary.
   - Ask your provider to address the specific medical-necessity criteria UnitedHealthcare cited and explain why the requested treatment was appropriate for your condition.

3. **Request expedited review if medically urgent**
   - External reviews are generally decided within **45 days**.
   - Expedited reviews may be decided within **72 hours** when waiting could seriously jeopardize your health.

UnitedHealthcare’s appeal form confirms that processed-claim appeals require details such as the provider, claim ID, date of service, and charge amount. HealthCare.gov also explains that medical-judgment denials are eligible for external review and that the insurer must accept the external reviewer’s decision.

You can start with UnitedHealthcare’s member appeal form:  
[UnitedHealthcare Member Service Request Form](https://memberforms.uhc.com/Memberappealsandgrievances.html)

If the final notice directs you to the federal process, HealthCare.gov lists [externalappeal.cms.gov](https://externalappeal.cms.gov/) as the preferred submission route. Also, contact the provider’s billing or utilization-review office—providers often submit the strongest clinical documentation and may help prepare the request.

Because your EOB shows **$1,280 in patient responsibility**, ask UnitedHealthcare and the provider whether billing can be placed on hold while the external review is pending. Keep copies and proof of submission.
